name: Barbara Carroll
abstract: Barbara Carroll (born Barbara Carole Coppersmith; January 25, 1925 – February 12, 2017) was an American jazz pianist and vocalist.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival New York: Barbara Carroll

works.t: Merrily We Roll Along: Old Friends
Porgy and Bess: Act I, Scene 2. My Man's Gone Now
Girl Crazy: But Not for Me
Show Boat: Nobody Else But Me

concerts.label: Michael Feinstein: Now and Then--Michael Feinstein and Friends
The Songs of Bart Howard and  Elizabeth Sullivan

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Jolly
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah it was Art Mardigan. Okay that was recorded again during that March period, ’55. That was the same time I did my first album. Yeah, boy, I’ve got this around some place, but Barbara Carroll, Quincy, Al Cohn, Ernie Wilkins, Andre, Billy Byers, Milt Bernhart, Joe Newman.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Jolly interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I remember talking to Barbara about this, and she said, “well I didn’t know it was a thing, we’ve just been playing and doing our thing right along.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: Now we were the headliners. Napoleon Brothers, and in fact Barbara Carroll, she was on the bill, one of the things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Barbara Carroll had been in there and Joe Shulman, who later married her.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: The people that were in my immediate circle . . . Barbara was one.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
